description: Deobandi jihadist militant organization primarily operating along the Afghanistan-Pakistan border. Founded by Baitullah Mehsud in 2007, it seeks to overthrow the Pakistani government and establish an Islamic state. Known for suicide bombings, sectarian violence, and attacks on security forces. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and several countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Pakistani Taliban have previously assisted the Afghan Taliban in the 2001–2021 war... The TTP depends on the tribal belt along the Afghanistan–Pakistan border... In 2019, there were around 3,000 to 4,000 TTP militants in Afghanistan...
country_links.context: The TTP operates from and carries out attacks across the Afghanistan–Pakistan border, with members based in Afghanistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: Pakistani officials allege that the TTP trained 85 French nationals between 2009 and 2012.
country_links.context: The TTP allegedly trained French nationals, including Mohammed Merah, in North Waziristan.

country_links.evidence_quote: The TTP released a video of Hakimullah Mehsud sitting next to the suicide bomber, Humam Khalil Abu Mulal al-Balawi, a Jordanian national who had been working with the CIA.
country_links.context: The TTP used a Jordanian national as a suicide bomber in the Camp Chapman attack in Afghanistan.
The TTP was involved in the 2008 Barcelona terror plot.

country_links.evidence_quote: the TTP's media wing, the Umar Media, had claimed to have set up training camps and to have sent hundreds of fighters to Syria to fight alongside the Syrian rebel groups...
country_links.context: The TTP set up training camps and sent fighters to Syria to fight alongside rebel groups.

country_links.evidence_quote: The TTP claimed responsibility for the December 2009 suicide attack on CIA facilities in Camp Chapman in Afghanistan, as well as the attempted bombing in Times Square in May 2010.
country_links.context: The TTP claimed responsibility for the attempted bombing in Times Square in the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: The TTP claimed responsibility for the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack, which was carried out jointly with the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan. The militants who participated in the attack were Uzbek foreigners.
country_links.context: The TTP carried out the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack jointly with the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan, using Uzbek militants.
